Linked List.title: Convert Sorted List to Binary Search Tree
Linked List.problem_statement: Given the head of a singly linked list where elements are sorted in ascending order, convert it to a height-balanced binary search tree.
constraints: The number of nodes in head is in the range [0, 2 * 10^4].
Linked List.explanation: One possible answer is [0,-3,9,-10,null,5], which represents the balanced BST.

code.python:
class Solution:
    def sortedListToBST(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[TreeNode]:
        if not head: return None
        if not head.next: return TreeNode(head.val)
        slow, fast, prev = head, head, None
        while fast and fast.next:
            prev = slow
            slow = slow.next
            fast = fast.next.next
        prev.next = None
        root = TreeNode(slow.val)
        root.left = self.sortedListToBST(head)
        root.right = self.sortedListToBST(slow.next)
        return root

Queue.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
Queue.problem_statement: Implement a first in first out (FIFO) queue using only two stacks. The implemented queue should support all the functions of a normal queue (push, peek, pop, and empty).
Queue.constraints: 1 <= x <= 9, At most 100 calls will be made to push, pop, peek, and empty.
Queue.explanation: MyQueue myQueue = new MyQueue(); myQueue.push(1); myQueue.push(2); myQueue.peek(); // return 1, myQueue.pop(); // return 1, myQueue.empty(); // return False

code.cpp: class MyQueue { stack<int> s1, s2; public: MyQueue() {} void push(int x) { s1.push(x); } int pop() { peek(); int res = s2.top(); s2.pop(); return res; } int peek() { if(s2.empty()) { while(!s1.empty()) { s2.push(s1.top()); s1.pop(); } } return s2.top(); } bool empty() { return s1.empty() && s2.empty(); } };
code.java: class MyQueue { Stack<Integer> s1 = new Stack<>(); Stack<Integer> s2 = new Stack<>(); public MyQueue() {} public void push(int x) { s1.push(x); } public int pop() { peek(); return s2.pop(); } public int peek() { if (s2.empty()) { while (!s1.empty()) { s2.push(s1.pop()); } } return s2.peek(); } public boolean empty() { return s1.empty() && s2.empty(); } }

Queue.title: Design Circular Queue
Queue.problem_statement: Design your implementation of the circular queue. The circular queue is a linear data structure in which the operations are performed based on FIFO principle and the last position is connected back to the first position to make a circle.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= k <= 1000, 0 <= value <= 1000, At most 3000 calls will be made to all functions.
Queue.sample_output: [null, true, true, true, false, 3, true, true, true, 4]
Queue.explanation: MyCircularQueue myCircularQueue = new MyCircularQueue(3); myCircularQueue.enQueue(1); // return True

code.cpp: class MyCircularQueue { vector<int> q; int head=0, tail=-1, len=0, cap; public: MyCircularQueue(int k) { q.resize(k); cap=k; } bool enQueue(int value) { if(isFull()) return false; tail = (tail+1)%cap; q[tail]=value; len++; return true; } bool deQueue() { if(isEmpty()) return false; head = (head+1)%cap; len--; return true; } int Front() { return isEmpty()?-1:q[head]; } int Rear() { return isEmpty()?-1:q[tail]; } bool isEmpty() { return len==0; } bool isFull() { return len==cap; } };
code.java: class MyCircularQueue { int[] q; int head = 0, tail = -1, len = 0; public MyCircularQueue(int k) { q = new int[k]; } public boolean enQueue(int value) { if (isFull()) return false; tail = (tail + 1) % q.length; q[tail] = value; len++; return true; } public boolean deQueue() { if (isEmpty()) return false; head = (head + 1) % q.length; len--; return true; } public int Front() { return isEmpty() ? -1 : q[head]; } public int Rear() { return isEmpty() ? -1 : q[tail]; } public boolean isEmpty() { return len == 0; } public boolean isFull() { return len == q.length; } }

Queue.title: Sliding Window Maximum
Queue.problem_statement: You are given an array of integers nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position. Return the max sliding window.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 10^5, -10^4 <= nums[i] <= 10^4, 1 <= k <= nums.length

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> maxSlidingWindow(vector<int>& nums, int k) { deque<int> dq; vector<int> res; for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); ++i) { if (!dq.empty() && dq.front() == i - k) dq.pop_front(); while (!dq.empty() && nums[dq.back()] < nums[i]) dq.pop_back(); dq.push_back(i); if (i >= k - 1) res.push_back(nums[dq.front()]); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] maxSlidingWindow(int[] nums, int k) { int n = nums.length; int[] res = new int[n - k + 1]; Deque<Integer> dq = new ArrayDeque<>(); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (!dq.isEmpty() && dq.peekFirst() == i - k) dq.pollFirst(); while (!dq.isEmpty() && nums[dq.peekLast()] < nums[i]) dq.pollLast(); dq.offerLast(i); if (i >= k - 1) res[i - k + 1] = nums[dq.peekFirst()]; } return res; } }

Queue.title: Number of Recent Calls
Queue.problem_statement: You have a RecentCounter class which counts the number of recent requests within a certain time frame.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= t <= 10^9, Each test case will call ping with strictly increasing values of t. At most 10^4 calls will be made to ping.
Queue.explanation: RecentCounter recentCounter = new RecentCounter(); recentCounter.ping(1); // requests = [1], range is [-2999,1], return 1

code.cpp: class RecentCounter { queue<int> q; public: RecentCounter() {} int ping(int t) { q.push(t); while(q.front() < t - 3000) q.pop(); return q.size(); } };
code.java: class RecentCounter { Queue<Integer> q; public RecentCounter() { q = new LinkedList<>(); } public int ping(int t) { q.add(t); while (q.peek() < t - 3000) q.poll(); return q.size(); } }

Queue.space_complexity: O(N) where N is number of recent pings

Queue.title: Design Circular Deque
Queue.problem_statement: Design your implementation of the circular double-ended queue (deque).
Queue.constraints: 1 <= k <= 1000, 0 <= value <= 1000, At most 2000 calls will be made to all functions.
Queue.sample_output: [null, true, true, true, false, 2, true, true, true, 4]
Queue.explanation: MyCircularDeque myCircularDeque = new MyCircularDeque(3); myCircularDeque.insertLast(1); // return True

code.cpp: class MyCircularDeque { vector<int> q; int front, rear, len, cap; public: MyCircularDeque(int k) { cap = k; len = 0; front = 0; rear = k - 1; q.resize(k); } bool insertFront(int value) { if(isFull()) return false; front = (front - 1 + cap) % cap; q[front] = value; len++; return true; } bool insertLast(int value) { if(isFull()) return false; rear = (rear + 1) % cap; q[rear] = value; len++; return true; } bool deleteFront() { if(isEmpty()) return false; front = (front + 1) % cap; len--; return true; } bool deleteLast() { if(isEmpty()) return false; rear = (rear - 1 + cap) % cap; len--; return true; } int getFront() { return isEmpty() ? -1 : q[front]; } int getRear() { return isEmpty() ? -1 : q[rear]; } bool isEmpty() { return len == 0; } bool isFull() { return len == cap; } };
code.java: class MyCircularDeque { int[] q; int front, rear, len, cap; public MyCircularDeque(int k) { cap = k; len = 0; front = 0; rear = k - 1; q = new int[k]; } public boolean insertFront(int value) { if(isFull()) return false; front = (front - 1 + cap) % cap; q[front] = value; len++; return true; } public boolean insertLast(int value) { if(isFull()) return false; rear = (rear + 1) % cap; q[rear] = value; len++; return true; } public boolean deleteFront() { if(isEmpty()) return false; front = (front + 1) % cap; len--; return true; } public boolean deleteLast() { if(isEmpty()) return false; rear = (rear - 1 + cap) % cap; len--; return true; } public int getFront() { return isEmpty() ? -1 : q[front]; } public int getRear() { return isEmpty() ? -1 : q[rear]; } public boolean isEmpty() { return len == 0; } public boolean isFull() { return len == cap; } }
code.python:
class MyCircularDeque:
    def __init__(self, k: int):
        self.cap = k
        self.len = 0
        self.front = 0
        self.rear = k - 1
        self.q = [0] * k
    def insertFront(self, value: int) -> bool:
        if self.isFull(): return False
        self.front = (self.front - 1) % self.cap
        self.q[self.front] = value
        self.len += 1
        return True
    def insertLast(self, value: int) -> bool:
        if self.isFull(): return False
        self.rear = (self.rear + 1) % self.cap
        self.q[self.rear] = value
        self.len += 1
        return True
    def deleteFront(self) -> bool:
        if self.isEmpty(): return False
        self.front = (self.front + 1) % self.cap
        self.len -= 1
        return True
    def deleteLast(self) -> bool:
        if self.isEmpty(): return False
        self.rear = (self.rear - 1) % self.cap
        self.len -= 1
        return True
    def getFront(self) -> int:
        return -1 if self.isEmpty() else self.q[self.front]
    def getRear(self) -> int:
        return -1 if self.isEmpty() else self.q[self.rear]
    def isEmpty(self) -> bool:
        return self.len == 0
    def isFull(self) -> bool:
        return self.len == self.cap

Queue.title: Time Needed to Buy Tickets
Queue.problem_statement: There are n people in a line queuing to buy tickets, where the 0th person is at the front of the line and the (n - 1)th person is at the back of the line. Return the time taken for the person at position k (0-indexed) to finish buying tickets.
Queue.constraints: n == tickets.length, 1 <= n <= 100, 1 <= tickets[i] <= 100, 0 <= k < n
Queue.explanation: The queue starts as [2,3,2], where the kth person is underlined. The person at the front has 2 tickets, buys one and goes to the back.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int timeRequiredToBuy(vector<int>& tickets, int k) { int res = 0; for(int i=0; i<tickets.size(); i++) { if(i <= k) res += min(tickets[i], tickets[k]); else res += min(tickets[i], tickets[k] - 1); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int timeRequiredToBuy(int[] tickets, int k) { int res = 0; for(int i=0; i<tickets.length; i++) { if(i <= k) res += Math.min(tickets[i], tickets[k]); else res += Math.min(tickets[i], tickets[k] - 1); } return res; } }

Queue.title: Reveal Cards In Increasing Order
Queue.problem_statement: You are given an integer array deck. There is a deck of cards where every card has a unique integer. You can order the deck in any order you want.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= deck.length <= 1000, 1 <= deck[i] <= 10^6, All the values of deck are unique.
Queue.explanation: We get the deck in the order [2,13,3,11,5,17,7] (this is what the output is), and we process it by taking the top, revealing it, and putting the next at the bottom.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> deckRevealedIncreasing(vector<int>& deck) { sort(deck.begin(), deck.end()); deque<int> dq; for(int i=deck.size()-1; i>=0; i--) { if(!dq.empty()) { dq.push_front(dq.back()); dq.pop_back(); } dq.push_front(deck[i]); } return vector<int>(dq.begin(), dq.end()); } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] deckRevealedIncreasing(int[] deck) { Arrays.sort(deck); Deque<Integer> dq = new LinkedList<>(); for (int i = deck.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) { if (!dq.isEmpty()) { dq.addFirst(dq.pollLast()); } dq.addFirst(deck[i]); } int[] res = new int[deck.length]; int i = 0; for (int num : dq) { res[i++] = num; } return res; } }

Queue.title: Find the Winner of the Circular Game
Queue.problem_statement: There are n friends that are playing a game. The friends are sitting in a circle and are numbered from 1 to n in clockwise order. Return the winner of the game.
Queue.explanation: Here are the steps of the game: 1) Start at friend 1. 2) Count 2 friends clockwise, which are friends 1 and 2. 3) Friend 2 leaves the circle.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int findTheWinner(int n, int k) { int res = 0; for (int i = 1; i <= n; ++i) res = (res + k) % i; return res + 1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int findTheWinner(int n, int k) { int res = 0; for (int i = 1; i <= n; ++i) res = (res + k) % i; return res + 1; } }

Queue.title: First Unique Character in a String
Queue.problem_statement: Given a string s, find the first non-repeating character in it and return its index. If it does not exist, return -1.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 10^5, s consists of only lowercase English letters.
Queue.explanation: The first non-repeating character is 'l', which is at index 0.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int firstUniqChar(string s) { vector<int> count(26, 0); for (char c : s) count[c - 'a']++; for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) { if (count[s[i] - 'a'] == 1) return i; } return -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int firstUniqChar(String s) { int[] count = new int[26]; for (char c : s.toCharArray()) count[c - 'a']++; for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) { if (count[s.charAt(i) - 'a'] == 1) return i; } return -1; } }

Queue.title: Implement Stack using Queues
Queue.problem_statement: Implement a last-in-first-out (LIFO) stack using only two queues. The implemented stack should support all the functions of a normal stack (push, top, pop, and empty).
Queue.constraints: 1 <= x <= 9, At most 100 calls will be made to push, pop, top, and empty.
Queue.explanation: MyStack myStack = new MyStack(); myStack.push(1); myStack.push(2); myStack.top(); // return 2

code.cpp: class MyStack { queue<int> q; public: MyStack() {} void push(int x) { q.push(x); for(int i=0; i<q.size()-1; i++) { q.push(q.front()); q.pop(); } } int pop() { int res = q.front(); q.pop(); return res; } int top() { return q.front(); } bool empty() { return q.empty(); } };
code.java: class MyStack { Queue<Integer> q = new LinkedList<>(); public MyStack() {} public void push(int x) { q.add(x); for(int i=0; i<q.size()-1; i++) { q.add(q.poll()); } } public int pop() { return q.poll(); } public int top() { return q.peek(); } public boolean empty() { return q.isEmpty(); } }

Queue.time_complexity: O(N) for push, O(1) for pop

Queue.title: Moving Average from Data Stream
Queue.problem_statement: Given a stream of integers and a window size, calculate the moving average of all integers in the sliding window.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= size <= 1000, -10^5 <= val <= 10^5, At most 10^4 calls will be made to next.
Queue.explanation: MovingAverage movingAverage = new MovingAverage(3); movingAverage.next(1); // return 1.0

code.cpp: class MovingAverage { queue<int> q; int size; double sum; public: MovingAverage(int size) { this->size = size; sum = 0; } double next(int val) { q.push(val); sum += val; if (q.size() > size) { sum -= q.front(); q.pop(); } return sum / q.size(); } };
code.java: class MovingAverage { Queue<Integer> q; int size; double sum; public MovingAverage(int size) { this.q = new LinkedList<>(); this.size = size; this.sum = 0; } public double next(int val) { q.add(val); sum += val; if (q.size() > size) { sum -= q.poll(); } return sum / q.size(); } }

Queue.title: Design Hit Counter
Queue.problem_statement: Design a hit counter which counts the number of hits received in the past 5 minutes (i.e., the past 300 seconds).
Queue.constraints: 1 <= timestamp <= 2 * 10^9, All calls to hit and getHits are in strictly increasing order of time. At most 300 calls will be made.
Queue.explanation: HitCounter hitCounter = new HitCounter(); hitCounter.hit(1); // hit at timestamp 1.

code.cpp: class HitCounter { queue<int> q; public: HitCounter() {} void hit(int timestamp) { q.push(timestamp); } int getHits(int timestamp) { while(!q.empty() && timestamp - q.front() >= 300) q.pop(); return q.size(); } };
code.java: class HitCounter { Queue<Integer> q; public HitCounter() { q = new LinkedList<>(); } public void hit(int timestamp) { q.add(timestamp); } public int getHits(int timestamp) { while(!q.isEmpty() && timestamp - q.peek() >= 300) q.poll(); return q.size(); } }

Queue.title: Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit
Queue.problem_statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer limit, return the size of the longest non-empty subarray such that the absolute difference between any two elements of this subarray is less than or equal to limit.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int longestSubarray(vector<int>& nums, int limit) { deque<int> maxd, mind; int i = 0, j; for(j = 0; j < nums.size(); j++) { while(!maxd.empty() && nums[j] > maxd.back()) maxd.pop_back(); while(!mind.empty() && nums[j] < mind.back()) mind.pop_back(); maxd.push_back(nums[j]); mind.push_back(nums[j]); if(maxd.front() - mind.front() > limit) { if(maxd.front() == nums[i]) maxd.pop_front(); if(mind.front() == nums[i]) mind.pop_front(); ++i; } } return j - i; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int longestSubarray(int[] nums, int limit) { Deque<Integer> maxd = new ArrayDeque<>(); Deque<Integer> mind = new ArrayDeque<>(); int i = 0, j; for (j = 0; j < nums.length; j++) { while (!maxd.isEmpty() && nums[j] > maxd.peekLast()) maxd.pollLast(); while (!mind.isEmpty() && nums[j] < mind.peekLast()) mind.pollLast(); maxd.offerLast(nums[j]); mind.offerLast(nums[j]); if (maxd.peekFirst() - mind.peekFirst() > limit) { if (maxd.peekFirst() == nums[i]) maxd.pollFirst(); if (mind.peekFirst() == nums[i]) mind.pollFirst(); ++i; } } return j - i; } }

Queue.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K
Queue.problem_statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the length of the shortest non-empty subarray of nums with a sum of at least k. If there is no such subarray, return -1.
Queue.explanation: The subarray [2,-1,2] has sum 3 >= 3.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int shortestSubarray(vector<int>& nums, int k) { int n = nums.size(), res = n + 1; vector<long> p(n + 1); for(int i=0; i<n; i++) p[i+1] = p[i] + nums[i]; deque<int> dq; for(int i=0; i<=n; i++) { while(!dq.empty() && p[i] - p[dq.front()] >= k) { res = min(res, i - dq.front()); dq.pop_front(); } while(!dq.empty() && p[i] <= p[dq.back()]) dq.pop_back(); dq.push_back(i); } return res <= n ? res : -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int shortestSubarray(int[] nums, int k) { int n = nums.length, res = n + 1; long[] p = new long[n + 1]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) p[i + 1] = p[i] + nums[i]; Deque<Integer> dq = new ArrayDeque<>(); for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) { while (!dq.isEmpty() && p[i] - p[dq.peekFirst()] >= k) { res = Math.min(res, i - dq.pollFirst()); } while (!dq.isEmpty() && p[i] <= p[dq.peekLast()]) dq.pollLast(); dq.offerLast(i); } return res <= n ? res : -1; } }

Queue.title: Jump Game VI
Queue.problem_statement: You are given a 0-indexed integer array nums and an integer k. You are initially standing at index 0. In one move, you can jump at most k steps forward. Return the maximum score you can get.
Queue.explanation: You can choose your jumps forming the subsequence [1,-1,4,3] with sum = 7.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int maxResult(vector<int>& nums, int k) { deque<int> dq; dq.push_back(0); for (int i = 1; i < nums.size(); ++i) { if (dq.front() < i - k) dq.pop_front(); nums[i] += nums[dq.front()]; while (!dq.empty() && nums[dq.back()] <= nums[i]) dq.pop_back(); dq.push_back(i); } return nums.back(); } };
code.java: class Solution { public int maxResult(int[] nums, int k) { Deque<Integer> dq = new LinkedList<>(); dq.offer(0); for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; ++i) { if (dq.peekFirst() < i - k) dq.pollFirst(); nums[i] += nums[dq.peekFirst()]; while (!dq.isEmpty() && nums[dq.peekLast()] <= nums[i]) dq.pollLast(); dq.offerLast(i); } return nums[nums.length - 1]; } }

Queue.title: Constrained Subsequence Sum
Queue.problem_statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the maximum sum of a non-empty subsequence of that array such that for every two consecutive integers in the subsequence, nums[i] and nums[j], where i < j, the condition j - i <= k is satisfied.
Queue.explanation: The subsequence is [10, 2, 5, 20].

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int constrainedSubsetSum(vector<int>& nums, int k) { deque<int> dq; int res = nums[0]; for(int i=0; i<nums.size(); i++) { if(!dq.empty()) nums[i] += nums[dq.front()]; res = max(res, nums[i]); while(!dq.empty() && nums[i] >= nums[dq.back()]) dq.pop_back(); if(nums[i] > 0) dq.push_back(i); if(!dq.empty() && dq.front() <= i - k) dq.pop_front(); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int constrainedSubsetSum(int[] nums, int k) { Deque<Integer> dq = new LinkedList<>(); int res = nums[0]; for(int i=0; i<nums.length; i++) { if(!dq.isEmpty()) nums[i] += nums[dq.peekFirst()]; res = Math.max(res, nums[i]); while(!dq.isEmpty() && nums[i] >= nums[dq.peekLast()]) dq.pollLast(); if(nums[i] > 0) dq.offerLast(i); if(!dq.isEmpty() && dq.peekFirst() <= i - k) dq.pollFirst(); } return res; } }

Queue.title: Maximum Number of Robots Within Budget
Queue.problem_statement: You have n robots. You are given two 0-indexed integer arrays, chargeTimes and runningCosts. You want to run as many consecutive robots as possible within budget.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= n <= 10^5, 1 <= chargeTimes[i], runningCosts[i] <= 10^5, 1 <= budget <= 10^14
Queue.explanation: It is possible to run all individual and consecutive pairs of robots within budget.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int maximumRobots(vector<int>& chargeTimes, vector<int>& runningCosts, long long budget) { deque<int> dq; long long sum = 0; int i = 0, n = chargeTimes.size(); for(int j=0; j<n; j++) { sum += runningCosts[j]; while(!dq.empty() && chargeTimes[dq.back()] <= chargeTimes[j]) dq.pop_back(); dq.push_back(j); if(chargeTimes[dq.front()] + (j - i + 1) * sum > budget) { sum -= runningCosts[i]; if(dq.front() == i) dq.pop_front(); ++i; } } return n - i; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int maximumRobots(int[] chargeTimes, int[] runningCosts, long budget) { Deque<Integer> dq = new ArrayDeque<>(); long sum = 0; int i = 0, n = chargeTimes.length; for(int j=0; j<n; j++) { sum += runningCosts[j]; while(!dq.isEmpty() && chargeTimes[dq.peekLast()] <= chargeTimes[j]) dq.pollLast(); dq.offerLast(j); if(chargeTimes[dq.peekFirst()] + (j - i + 1) * sum > budget) { sum -= runningCosts[i]; if(dq.peekFirst() == i) dq.pollFirst(); ++i; } } return n - i; } }

Queue.title: Task Scheduler
Queue.problem_statement: Given a characters array tasks, representing the tasks a CPU needs to do, where each letter represents a different task. Tasks could be done in any order. Each task is done in one unit of time. For each unit of time, the CPU could complete either one task or just be idle.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= task.length <= 10^4, tasks[i] is upper-case English letter. The integer n is in the range [0, 100].
Queue.explanation: A -> B -> idle -> A -> B -> idle -> A -> B

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int leastInterval(vector<char>& tasks, int n) { unordered_map<char, int> mp; int max_freq = 0; for(char c : tasks) { mp[c]++; max_freq = max(max_freq, mp[c]); } int max_count = 0; for(auto p : mp) { if(p.second == max_freq) max_count++; } return max((int)tasks.size(), (max_freq - 1) * (n + 1) + max_count); } };
code.java: class Solution { public int leastInterval(char[] tasks, int n) { int[] freq = new int[26]; int maxFreq = 0; for(char c : tasks) { freq[c-'A']++; maxFreq = Math.max(maxFreq, freq[c-'A']); } int maxCount = 0; for(int f : freq) { if(f == maxFreq) maxCount++; } return Math.max(tasks.length, (maxFreq - 1) * (n + 1) + maxCount); } }

Queue.title: Open the Lock
Queue.problem_statement: You have a lock in front of you with 4 circular wheels. Each wheel has 10 slots. The lock initially starts at '0000', a string representing the state of the 4 wheels. Find the minimum total number of turns required to open the lock, or -1 if it is impossible.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= deadends.length <= 500, deadends[i].length == 4, target.length == 4

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int openLock(vector<string>& deadends, string target) { unordered_set<string> dead(deadends.begin(), deadends.end()); if(dead.count("0000")) return -1; queue<string> q; q.push("0000"); dead.insert("0000"); int steps = 0; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { string curr = q.front(); q.pop(); if(curr == target) return steps; for(int i=0; i<4; i++) { for(int d : {-1, 1}) { string next = curr; next[i] = (next[i] - '0' + d + 10) % 10 + '0'; if(!dead.count(next)) { dead.insert(next); q.push(next); } } } } steps++; } return -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int openLock(String[] deadends, String target) { Set<String> dead = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(deadends)); if(dead.contains("0000")) return -1; Queue<String> q = new LinkedList<>(); q.offer("0000"); dead.add("0000"); int steps = 0; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { String curr = q.poll(); if(curr.equals(target)) return steps; for(int i=0; i<4; i++) { for(int d : new int[]{-1, 1}) { char[] chars = curr.toCharArray(); chars[i] = (char)(((chars[i] - '0' + d + 10) % 10) + '0'); String next = new String(chars); if(dead.add(next)) q.offer(next); } } } steps++; } return -1; } }

Queue.title: Number of Islands
Queue.problem_statement: Given an m x n 2D binary grid grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
Queue.constraints: m == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= m, n <= 300, grid[i][j] is '0' or '1'.
Queue.explanation: All '1's are connected.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int numIslands(vector<vector<char>>& grid) { int islands = 0; for(int i=0; i<grid.size(); i++) { for(int j=0; j<grid[0].size(); j++) { if(grid[i][j] == '1') { islands++; queue<pair<int,int>> q; q.push({i,j}); grid[i][j] = '0'; while(!q.empty()) { auto [r,c] = q.front(); q.pop(); int dirs[4][2] = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(auto& d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<grid.size() && nc>=0 && nc<grid[0].size() && grid[nr][nc]=='1') { grid[nr][nc]='0'; q.push({nr,nc}); } } } } } } return islands; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int numIslands(char[][] grid) { int islands = 0; for(int i=0; i<grid.length; i++) { for(int j=0; j<grid[0].length; j++) { if(grid[i][j] == '1') { islands++; Queue<int[]> q = new LinkedList<>(); q.offer(new int[]{i,j}); grid[i][j] = '0'; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int[] curr = q.poll(); int r=curr[0], c=curr[1]; int[][] dirs = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(int[] d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<grid.length && nc>=0 && nc<grid[0].length && grid[nr][nc]=='1') { grid[nr][nc]='0'; q.offer(new int[]{nr,nc}); } } } } } } return islands; } }

Queue.title: Perfect Squares
Queue.problem_statement: Given an integer n, return the least number of perfect square numbers that sum to n.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int numSquares(int n) { queue<int> q; unordered_set<int> visited; q.push(n); int level = 0; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); level++; while(size--) { int curr = q.front(); q.pop(); for(int i=1; i*i<=curr; i++) { int next = curr - i*i; if(next == 0) return level; if(!visited.count(next)) { visited.insert(next); q.push(next); } } } } return level; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int numSquares(int n) { Queue<Integer> q = new LinkedList<>(); Set<Integer> visited = new HashSet<>(); q.offer(n); int level = 0; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); level++; while(size-- > 0) { int curr = q.poll(); for(int i=1; i*i<=curr; i++) { int next = curr - i*i; if(next == 0) return level; if(visited.add(next)) { q.offer(next); } } } } return level; } }

Queue.title: Word Ladder
Queue.problem_statement: A transformation sequence from word beginWord to word endWord using a dictionary wordList is a sequence of words beginWord -> s1 -> s2 -> ... -> sk such that every adjacent pair of words differs by a single letter.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= beginWord.length <= 10, endWord.length == beginWord.length, 1 <= wordList.length <= 5000
Queue.explanation: One shortest transformation sequence is "hit" -> "hot" -> "dot" -> "dog" -> "cog", which is 5 words long.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int ladderLength(string beginWord, string endWord, vector<string>& wordList) { unordered_set<string> words(wordList.begin(), wordList.end()); if(!words.count(endWord)) return 0; queue<string> q; q.push(beginWord); int steps = 1; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { string curr = q.front(); q.pop(); if(curr == endWord) return steps; for(int i=0; i<curr.length(); i++) { char orig = curr[i]; for(char c='a'; c<='z'; c++) { curr[i] = c; if(words.count(curr)) { q.push(curr); words.erase(curr); } } curr[i] = orig; } } steps++; } return 0; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int ladderLength(String beginWord, String endWord, List<String> wordList) { Set<String> words = new HashSet<>(wordList); if(!words.contains(endWord)) return 0; Queue<String> q = new LinkedList<>(); q.offer(beginWord); int steps = 1; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { String curr = q.poll(); if(curr.equals(endWord)) return steps; char[] chars = curr.toCharArray(); for(int i=0; i<chars.length; i++) { char orig = chars[i]; for(char c='a'; c<='z'; c++) { chars[i] = c; String next = new String(chars); if(words.contains(next)) { q.offer(next); words.remove(next); } } chars[i] = orig; } } steps++; } return 0; } }

Queue.title: Minimum Genetic Mutation
Queue.problem_statement: A gene string can be represented by an 8-character long string, with choices from 'A', 'C', 'G', and 'T'. Given the two gene strings startGene and endGene and the gene bank bank, return the minimum number of mutations needed to mutate from startGene to endGene.
Queue.constraints: 0 <= bank.length <= 10, startGene.length == endGene.length == bank[i].length == 8
Queue.explanation: Only one mutation is needed to change AACCGGTT to AACCGGTA.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int minMutation(string startGene, string endGene, vector<string>& bank) { unordered_set<string> valid(bank.begin(), bank.end()); if(!valid.count(endGene)) return -1; queue<string> q; q.push(startGene); int steps = 0; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { string curr = q.front(); q.pop(); if(curr == endGene) return steps; for(int i=0; i<8; i++) { char orig = curr[i]; for(char c : {'A', 'C', 'G', 'T'}) { curr[i] = c; if(valid.count(curr)) { q.push(curr); valid.erase(curr); } } curr[i] = orig; } } steps++; } return -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int minMutation(String startGene, String endGene, String[] bank) { Set<String> valid = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(bank)); if(!valid.contains(endGene)) return -1; Queue<String> q = new LinkedList<>(); q.offer(startGene); int steps = 0; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { String curr = q.poll(); if(curr.equals(endGene)) return steps; char[] chars = curr.toCharArray(); for(int i=0; i<8; i++) { char orig = chars[i]; for(char c : new char[]{'A', 'C', 'G', 'T'}) { chars[i] = c; String next = new String(chars); if(valid.contains(next)) { q.offer(next); valid.remove(next); } } chars[i] = orig; } } steps++; } return -1; } }

Queue.title: Shortest Path in Binary Matrix
Queue.problem_statement: Given an n x n binary matrix grid, return the length of the shortest clear path in the matrix. If there is no clear path, return -1. A clear path is a path from the top-left to the bottom-right cell such that all visited cells are 0. You can move 8-directionally.
Queue.constraints: n == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= n <= 100, grid[i][j] is 0 or 1
Queue.explanation: The shortest clear path is from (0,0) to (1,1).

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int shortestPathBinaryMatrix(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { int n = grid.size(); if(grid[0][0] || grid[n-1][n-1]) return -1; queue<pair<int,int>> q; q.push({0,0}); grid[0][0] = 1; int steps = 1; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { auto [r,c] = q.front(); q.pop(); if(r == n-1 && c == n-1) return steps; for(int i=-1; i<=1; i++) { for(int j=-1; j<=1; j++) { if(i==0 && j==0) continue; int nr = r + i, nc = c + j; if(nr>=0 && nr<n && nc>=0 && nc<n && !grid[nr][nc]) { grid[nr][nc] = 1; q.push({nr,nc}); } } } } steps++; } return -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int shortestPathBinaryMatrix(int[][] grid) { int n = grid.length; if(grid[0][0] == 1 || grid[n-1][n-1] == 1) return -1; Queue<int[]> q = new LinkedList<>(); q.offer(new int[]{0,0}); grid[0][0] = 1; int steps = 1; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { int[] curr = q.poll(); int r = curr[0], c = curr[1]; if(r == n-1 && c == n-1) return steps; for(int i=-1; i<=1; i++) { for(int j=-1; j<=1; j++) { if(i==0 && j==0) continue; int nr = r + i, nc = c + j; if(nr>=0 && nr<n && nc>=0 && nc<n && grid[nr][nc] == 0) { grid[nr][nc] = 1; q.offer(new int[]{nr,nc}); } } } } steps++; } return -1; } }

Queue.title: Rotting Oranges
Queue.problem_statement: You are given an m x n grid where each cell can have one of three values: 0 representing an empty cell, 1 representing a fresh orange, or 2 representing a rotten orange. Every minute, any fresh orange that is 4-directionally adjacent to a rotten orange becomes rotten. Return the minimum number of minutes that must elapse until no cell has a fresh orange.
Queue.constraints: m == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= m, n <= 10
Queue.explanation: It takes 4 minutes for all fresh oranges to become rotten.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int orangesRotting(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { queue<pair<int,int>> q; int fresh = 0, mins = -1; for(int i=0; i<grid.size(); i++) { for(int j=0; j<grid[0].size(); j++) { if(grid[i][j] == 2) q.push({i,j}); else if(grid[i][j] == 1) fresh++; } } if(fresh == 0) return 0; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { auto [r,c] = q.front(); q.pop(); int dirs[4][2] = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(auto& d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<grid.size() && nc>=0 && nc<grid[0].size() && grid[nr][nc] == 1) { grid[nr][nc] = 2; fresh--; q.push({nr,nc}); } } } mins++; } return fresh == 0 ? mins : -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int orangesRotting(int[][] grid) { Queue<int[]> q = new LinkedList<>(); int fresh = 0, mins = -1; for(int i=0; i<grid.length; i++) { for(int j=0; j<grid[0].length; j++) { if(grid[i][j] == 2) q.offer(new int[]{i,j}); else if(grid[i][j] == 1) fresh++; } } if(fresh == 0) return 0; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { int[] curr = q.poll(); int r = curr[0], c = curr[1]; int[][] dirs = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(int[] d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<grid.length && nc>=0 && nc<grid[0].length && grid[nr][nc] == 1) { grid[nr][nc] = 2; fresh--; q.offer(new int[]{nr,nc}); } } } mins++; } return fresh == 0 ? mins : -1; } }

Queue.title: 01 Matrix
Queue.problem_statement: Given an m x n binary matrix mat, return the distance of the nearest 0 for each cell. The distance between two adjacent cells is 1.
Queue.constraints: m == mat.length, n == mat[i].length, 1 <= m, n <= 10^4
Queue.explanation: All 1s are adjacent to at least one 0.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<vector<int>> updateMatrix(vector<vector<int>>& mat) { int m = mat.size(), n = mat[0].size(); queue<pair<int,int>> q; vector<vector<int>> dist(m, vector<int>(n, -1)); for(int i=0; i<m; i++) { for(int j=0; j<n; j++) { if(mat[i][j] == 0) { q.push({i,j}); dist[i][j] = 0; } } } while(!q.empty()) { auto [r,c] = q.front(); q.pop(); int dirs[4][2] = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(auto& d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<m && nc>=0 && nc<n && dist[nr][nc] == -1) { dist[nr][nc] = dist[r][c] + 1; q.push({nr,nc}); } } } return dist; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[][] updateMatrix(int[][] mat) { int m = mat.length, n = mat[0].length; Queue<int[]> q = new LinkedList<>(); int[][] dist = new int[m][n]; for(int i=0; i<m; i++) { for(int j=0; j<n; j++) { if(mat[i][j] == 0) { q.offer(new int[]{i,j}); } else { dist[i][j] = -1; } } } while(!q.isEmpty()) { int[] curr = q.poll(); int r = curr[0], c = curr[1]; int[][] dirs = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(int[] d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<m && nc>=0 && nc<n && dist[nr][nc] == -1) { dist[nr][nc] = dist[r][c] + 1; q.offer(new int[]{nr,nc}); } } } return dist; } }

Queue.title: As Far from Land as Possible
Queue.problem_statement: Given an n x n grid containing only values 0 and 1, where 0 represents water and 1 represents land, find a water cell such that its distance to the nearest land cell is maximized, and return the distance.
Queue.constraints: n == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= n <= 100, grid[i][j] is 0 or 1
Queue.explanation: The cell (1, 1) is as far as possible from all the land with distance 2.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int maxDistance(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { int n = grid.size(); queue<pair<int,int>> q; for(int i=0; i<n; i++) { for(int j=0; j<n; j++) { if(grid[i][j] == 1) q.push({i,j}); } } if(q.size() == 0 || q.size() == n*n) return -1; int dist = -1; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { auto [r,c] = q.front(); q.pop(); int dirs[4][2] = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(auto& d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<n && nc>=0 && nc<n && grid[nr][nc] == 0) { grid[nr][nc] = 1; q.push({nr,nc}); } } } dist++; } return dist; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int maxDistance(int[][] grid) { int n = grid.length; Queue<int[]> q = new LinkedList<>(); for(int i=0; i<n; i++) { for(int j=0; j<n; j++) { if(grid[i][j] == 1) q.offer(new int[]{i,j}); } } if(q.size() == 0 || q.size() == n*n) return -1; int dist = -1; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { int[] curr = q.poll(); int r = curr[0], c = curr[1]; int[][] dirs = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(int[] d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<n && nc>=0 && nc<n && grid[nr][nc] == 0) { grid[nr][nc] = 1; q.offer(new int[]{nr,nc}); } } } dist++; } return dist; } }

Queue.title: Shortest Bridge
Queue.problem_statement: You are given an n x n binary matrix grid where 1 represents land and 0 represents water. An island is a 4-directionally connected group of 1's not connected to any other 1's. There are exactly two islands in grid. Return the smallest number of 0's you must flip to connect the two islands.
Queue.constraints: n == grid.length == grid[i].length, 2 <= n <= 100
Queue.explanation: Flip one 0 to 1 to connect the two islands.

code.cpp: class Solution { void dfs(vector<vector<int>>& grid, int r, int c, queue<pair<int,int>>& q) { if(r<0 || r>=grid.size() || c<0 || c>=grid[0].size() || grid[r][c] != 1) return; grid[r][c] = 2; q.push({r,c}); dfs(grid, r+1, c, q); dfs(grid, r-1, c, q); dfs(grid, r, c+1, q); dfs(grid, r, c-1, q); } public: int shortestBridge(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { queue<pair<int,int>> q; bool found = false; for(int i=0; i<grid.size() && !found; i++) { for(int j=0; j<grid[0].size() && !found; j++) { if(grid[i][j] == 1) { dfs(grid, i, j, q); found = true; } } } int steps = 0; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { auto [r,c] = q.front(); q.pop(); int dirs[4][2] = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(auto& d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<grid.size() && nc>=0 && nc<grid[0].size()) { if(grid[nr][nc] == 1) return steps; if(grid[nr][nc] == 0) { grid[nr][nc] = 2; q.push({nr,nc}); } } } } steps++; } return -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { void dfs(int[][] grid, int r, int c, Queue<int[]> q) { if(r<0 || r>=grid.length || c<0 || c>=grid[0].length || grid[r][c] != 1) return; grid[r][c] = 2; q.offer(new int[]{r,c}); dfs(grid, r+1, c, q); dfs(grid, r-1, c, q); dfs(grid, r, c+1, q); dfs(grid, r, c-1, q); } public int shortestBridge(int[][] grid) { Queue<int[]> q = new LinkedList<>(); boolean found = false; for(int i=0; i<grid.length && !found; i++) { for(int j=0; j<grid[0].length && !found; j++) { if(grid[i][j] == 1) { dfs(grid, i, j, q); found = true; } } } int steps = 0; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { int[] curr = q.poll(); int r = curr[0], c = curr[1]; int[][] dirs = {{1,0},{-1,0},{0,1},{0,-1}}; for(int[] d : dirs) { int nr = r + d[0], nc = c + d[1]; if(nr>=0 && nr<grid.length && nc>=0 && nc<grid[0].length) { if(grid[nr][nc] == 1) return steps; if(grid[nr][nc] == 0) { grid[nr][nc] = 2; q.offer(new int[]{nr,nc}); } } } } steps++; } return -1; } }

Queue.title: Bus Routes
Queue.problem_statement: You are given an array routes representing bus routes where routes[i] is a bus route that the ith bus repeats forever. You will start at the bus stop source (You are not on any bus initially), and you want to go to the bus stop target. Return the least number of buses you must take to travel from source to target. Return -1 if it is not possible.
Queue.constraints: 1 <= routes.length <= 500, 1 <= routes[i].length <= 10^5, All the values of routes[i] are unique.
Queue.explanation: The best strategy is take the first bus to the bus stop 7, then take the second bus to the bus stop 6.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int numBusesToDestination(vector<vector<int>>& routes, int source, int target) { if(source == target) return 0; unordered_map<int, vector<int>> adj; for(int i=0; i<routes.size(); i++) { for(int stop : routes[i]) adj[stop].push_back(i); } queue<int> q; unordered_set<int> visited_routes; q.push(source); int buses = 0; while(!q.empty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size--) { int stop = q.front(); q.pop(); if(stop == target) return buses; for(int route : adj[stop]) { if(!visited_routes.count(route)) { visited_routes.insert(route); for(int next_stop : routes[route]) { q.push(next_stop); } } } } buses++; } return -1; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int numBusesToDestination(int[][] routes, int source, int target) { if(source == target) return 0; Map<Integer, List<Integer>> adj = new HashMap<>(); for(int i=0; i<routes.length; i++) { for(int stop : routes[i]) { adj.computeIfAbsent(stop, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(i); } } Queue<Integer> q = new LinkedList<>(); Set<Integer> visited_routes = new HashSet<>(); q.offer(source); int buses = 0; while(!q.isEmpty()) { int size = q.size(); while(size-- > 0) { int stop = q.poll(); if(stop == target) return buses; for(int route : adj.getOrDefault(stop, new ArrayList<>())) { if(visited_routes.add(route)) { for(int next_stop : routes[route]) { q.offer(next_stop); } } } } buses++; } return -1; } }

Queue.title: Sliding Window Median
Queue.problem_statement: The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. If the size of the list is even, there is no middle value. So the median is the mean of the two middle values. Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the median array for each window in the original array.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<double> medianSlidingWindow(vector<int>& nums, int k) { multiset<long long> lo, hi; vector<double> res; for(int i=0; i<nums.size(); i++) { if(lo.empty() || nums[i] <= *lo.rbegin()) lo.insert(nums[i]); else hi.insert(nums[i]); if(lo.size() > hi.size() + 1) { hi.insert(*lo.rbegin()); lo.erase(prev(lo.end())); } else if(hi.size() > lo.size()) { lo.insert(*hi.begin()); hi.erase(hi.begin()); } if(i >= k - 1) { res.push_back(k % 2 ? *lo.rbegin() : (*lo.rbegin() + *hi.begin()) / 2.0); int rem = nums[i - k + 1]; if(rem <= *lo.rbegin()) lo.erase(lo.find(rem)); else hi.erase(hi.find(rem)); if(lo.size() > hi.size() + 1) { hi.insert(*lo.rbegin()); lo.erase(prev(lo.end())); } else if(hi.size() > lo.size()) { lo.insert(*hi.begin()); hi.erase(hi.begin()); } } } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public double[] medianSlidingWindow(int[] nums, int k) { PriorityQueue<Long> lo = new PriorityQueue<>(Collections.reverseOrder()); PriorityQueue<Long> hi = new PriorityQueue<>(); double[] res = new double[nums.length - k + 1]; for(int i=0; i<nums.length; i++) { if(lo.isEmpty() || nums[i] <= lo.peek()) lo.offer((long)nums[i]); else hi.offer((long)nums[i]); if(lo.size() > hi.size() + 1) hi.offer(lo.poll()); else if(hi.size() > lo.size()) lo.offer(hi.poll()); if(i >= k - 1) { res[i - k + 1] = k % 2 != 0 ? lo.peek() : (lo.peek() + hi.peek()) / 2.0; long rem = nums[i - k + 1]; if(rem <= lo.peek()) lo.remove(rem); else hi.remove(rem); if(lo.size() > hi.size() + 1) hi.offer(lo.poll()); else if(hi.size() > lo.size()) lo.offer(hi.poll()); } } return res; } }

Sliding Window.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
Sliding Window.constraints:
0 <= s.length <= 5 * 10^4
s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces.
Sliding Window.explanation: The answer is "abc", with the length of 3.

Sliding Window.title: Minimum Window Substring
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If there is no such substring, return the empty string "".
Sliding Window.constraints:
m == s.length
n == t.length
1 <= m, n <= 10^5
s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters.
Sliding Window.explanation: The minimum window substring "BANC" includes 'A', 'B', and 'C' from string t.

Sliding Window.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an array of positive integers nums and a positive integer target, return the minimal length of a contiguous subarray [numsl, numsl+1, ..., numsr-1, numsr] of which the sum is greater than or equal to target. If there is no such subarray, return 0 instead.
Sliding Window.explanation: The subarray [4,3] has the minimal length under the problem constraint.

Sliding Window.title: Fruit Into Baskets
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are visiting a farm that has a single row of fruit trees arranged from left to right. You have two baskets, and each basket can hold an unlimited quantity of fruit, but each basket can only hold one type of fruit. Return the maximum number of fruits you can pick.
Sliding Window.explanation: We can pick from trees [2,3,2,2].

Sliding Window.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are given a string s and an integer k. You can choose any character of the string and change it to any other uppercase English character. You can perform this operation at most k times. Return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter you can get after performing the above operations.
Sliding Window.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^5
s consists of only uppercase English letters.
0 <= k <= s.length
Sliding Window.explanation: Replace the two 'A's with two 'B's or vice versa.

Sliding Window.title: Permutation in String
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. In other words, return true if one of s1's permutations is the substring of s2.
Sliding Window.constraints:
1 <= s1.length, s2.length <= 10^4
s1 and s2 consist of lowercase English letters.
Sliding Window.explanation: s2 contains one permutation of s1 ("ba").

Sliding Window.title: Find All Anagrams in a String
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given two strings s and p, return an array of all the start indices of p's anagrams in s. You may return the answer in any order.
Sliding Window.constraints:
1 <= s.length, p.length <= 3 * 10^4
s and p consist of lowercase English letters.
Sliding Window.explanation: The substring with start index = 0 is "cba", which is an anagram of "abc". The substring with start index = 6 is "bac", which is an anagram of "abc".

Sliding Window.title: Sliding Window Maximum
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are given an array of integers nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position. Return the max sliding window.

Sliding Window.title: Maximum Average Subarray I
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are given an integer array nums consisting of n elements, and an integer k. Find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value and return this value. Any answer with a calculation error less than 10^-5 will be accepted.
Sliding Window.explanation: Maximum average is (12 - 5 - 6 + 50) / 4 = 51 / 4 = 12.75

Sliding Window.title: Subarrays with K Different Integers
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the number of good subarrays of nums. A good array is an array where the number of different integers in that array is exactly k.
Sliding Window.explanation: Subarrays formed with exactly 2 different integers: [1,2], [2,1], [1,2], [2,3], [1,2,1], [2,1,2], [1,2,1,2]

Sliding Window.title: Max Consecutive Ones III
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given a binary array nums and an integer k, return the maximum number of consecutive 1's in the array if you can flip at most k 0's.
Sliding Window.constraints:
1 <= nums.length <= 10^5
nums[i] is either 0 or 1.
0 <= k <= nums.length
Sliding Window.explanation:
[1,1,1,0,0,1,1,1,1,1,1]
Bolded numbers were flipped from 0 to 1. The longest subarray is underlined.

Sliding Window.title: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given a string s and an integer k, return the length of the longest substring of s that contains at most k distinct characters.
Sliding Window.explanation: The substring is "ece" with length 3.

Sliding Window.title: Subarray Product Less Than K
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the number of contiguous subarrays where the product of all the elements in the subarray is strictly less than k.
Sliding Window.explanation: The 8 subarrays that have product less than 100 are: [10], [5], [2], [6], [10, 5], [5, 2], [2, 6], [5, 2, 6].

Sliding Window.title: Count Number of Nice Subarrays
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k. A continuous subarray is called nice if there are k odd numbers on it. Return the number of nice sub-arrays.
Sliding Window.explanation: The only sub-arrays with 3 odd numbers are [1,1,2,1] and [1,2,1,1].

Sliding Window.title: Replace the Substring for Balanced String
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are given a string s of length n containing only four kinds of characters: 'Q', 'W', 'E', and 'R'. A string is said to be balanced if each of its characters appears n/4 times where n is the length of the string. Return the minimum length of the substring that can be replaced with any other string of the same length to make s balanced.
Sliding Window.constraints:
n == s.length
4 <= n <= 10^5
n is a multiple of 4.
s contains only 'Q', 'W', 'E', and 'R'.
Sliding Window.explanation: s is already balanced.

Sliding Window.title: Maximize the Confusion of an Exam
Sliding Window.problem_statement: A teacher is writing a test with n true/false questions, with 'T' denoting true and 'F' denoting false. You can change the answer key for any question to 'T' or 'F' (i.e., set answerKey[i] to 'T' or 'F') at most k times. Return the maximum number of consecutive 'T's or 'F's in the answer key after performing the operation at most k times.
Sliding Window.constraints:
n == answerKey.length
1 <= n <= 5 * 10^4
answerKey[i] is either 'T' or 'F'
1 <= k <= n
Sliding Window.explanation: We can replace both the 'F's with 'T's to make answerKey = "TTTT".

Sliding Window.title: Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given a string s and an integer k, return the maximum number of vowel letters in any substring of s with length k. Vowel letters in English are 'a', 'e', 'i', 'o', and 'u'.
Sliding Window.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 10^5
s consists of lowercase English letters.
1 <= k <= s.length
Sliding Window.explanation: The substring "iii" contains 3 vowel letters.

Sliding Window.title: Maximum Points You Can Obtain from Cards
Sliding Window.problem_statement: There are several cards arranged in a row, and each card has an associated number of points. The points are given in the integer array cardPoints. In one step, you can take one card from the beginning or from the end of the row. You have to take exactly k cards. Your score is the sum of the points of the cards you have taken. Return the maximum score you can obtain.
Sliding Window.explanation: After the first step, your score will always be 1. However, choosing the rightmost card first will maximize your total score. The optimal strategy is to take the three cards on the right, giving a final score of 1 + 6 + 5 = 12.

Sliding Window.title: Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer limit, return the size of the longest non-empty subarray such that the absolute difference between any two elements of this subarray is less than or equal to limit.
Sliding Window.explanation:
All subarrays are: 
[8] with maximum absolute diff |8-8| = 0 <= 4.
[8,2] with maximum absolute diff |8-2| = 6 > 4. 
[8,2,4] with maximum absolute diff |8-2| = 6 > 4.
[8,2,4,7] with maximum absolute diff |8-2| = 6 > 4.
[2] with maximum absolute diff |2-2| = 0 <= 4.
[2,4] with maximum absolute diff |2-4| = 2 <= 4.
[2,4,7] with maximum absolute diff |2-7| = 5 > 4.
[4] with maximum absolute diff |4-4| = 0 <= 4.
[4,7] with maximum absolute diff |4-7| = 3 <= 4.
[7] with maximum absolute diff |7-7| = 0 <= 4. 
Therefore, the size of the longest subarray is 2.

Sliding Window.title: Minimum Swaps to Group All 1's Together II
Sliding Window.problem_statement: A swap is defined as taking two distinct positions in an array and swapping the values in them. A circular array is defined as an array where we consider the first element and the last element to be adjacent. Given a binary circular array nums, return the minimum number of swaps required to group all 1's present in the array together at any location.
Sliding Window.explanation:
Here are a few of the ways to group all the 1's together:
[0,0,1,1,1,0,0] using 1 swap.
[0,1,1,1,0,0,0] using 1 swap.
[1,1,0,0,0,0,1] using 2 swaps (using the circular property).
There is no way to group all 1's together with 0 swaps.
Thus, the minimum number of swaps required is 1.

Sliding Window.title: Sliding Window Median
Sliding Window.problem_statement: The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. If the size of the list is even, there is no middle value. So the median is the mean of the two middle values. You are given an integer array nums and an integer k. There is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. Return the median array for each window in the original array.
Sliding Window.time_complexity: O(N * K) or O(N log K)

Sliding Window.title: Find Two Non-overlapping Sub-arrays Each With Target Sum

There can be multiple answers so you have to find an answer where the sum of the lengths of the two sub-arrays is minimum. Return the minimum sum of the lengths of the two required sub-arrays, or return -1 if you cannot find such two sub-arrays.
Sliding Window.explanation: Only two sub-arrays have sum = 3 ([3] and [3]). The sum of their lengths is 2.

Sliding Window.title: Contains Duplicate II
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return true if there are two distinct indices i and j in the array such that nums[i] == nums[j] and abs(i - j) <= k.
Sliding Window.explanation: nums[0] == nums[3] and abs(0 - 3) = 3 <= k.

Sliding Window.title: Contains Duplicate III
