Sliding Window.explanation: We can choose (i, j) = (0, 3). We satisfy the conditions.

Sliding Window.title: Minimum Difference Between Highest and Lowest of K Scores
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are given a 0-indexed integer array nums, where nums[i] represents the score of the ith student. You are also given an integer k. Pick the scores of any k students from the array so that the difference between the highest and the lowest of the k scores is minimized. Return the minimum possible difference.
Sliding Window.explanation: There are six ways to pick score(s) of two students. The minimum difference is between 9 and 7: 9 - 7 = 2.

Sliding Window.title: Find the K-Beauty of a Number
Sliding Window.problem_statement: The k-beauty of an integer num is defined as the number of substrings of num when it is read as a string that meet the following conditions: It has a length of k. It is a divisor of num. Return the k-beauty of num.
Sliding Window.explanation: The substrings are "24" -> 24 divides 240. "40" -> 40 divides 240. 2 substrings.

Sliding Window.title: Number of Sub-arrays of Size K and Average Greater than or Equal to Threshold
Sliding Window.problem_statement: Given an array of integers arr and two integers k and threshold, return the number of sub-arrays of size k and average greater than or equal to threshold.
Sliding Window.explanation: Sub-arrays [2,5,5],[5,5,5] and [5,5,8] have averages 4, 5 and 6 respectively. All other sub-arrays of size 3 have averages less than 4.

Sliding Window.title: Maximum Erasure Value
Sliding Window.problem_statement: You are given an array of positive integers nums and want to erase a subarray containing unique elements. The score you get by erasing the subarray is equal to the sum of its elements. Return the maximum score you can get by erasing exactly one subarray.
Sliding Window.explanation: The optimal subarray here is [2,4,5,6].

Sliding Window.title: Substrings of Size Three with Distinct Characters
Sliding Window.problem_statement: A string is good if there are no repeated characters. Given a string s, return the number of good substrings of length three in s.
Sliding Window.constraints:
1 <= s.length <= 100
s consists of lowercase English letters.
Sliding Window.explanation: There are 4 substrings of size 3: "xyz", "yzz", "zza", and "zaz". The only good substring of length 3 is "xyz".

Sliding Window.title: Diet Plan Performance
Sliding Window.problem_statement: A dieter consumes calories[i] calories on the i-th day. For every consecutive sequence of k days, they look at T, the total calories consumed during that sequence of k days: If T < lower, they performed poorly on their diet and lose 1 point. If T > upper, they performed well on their diet and gain 1 point. Return their total points.
Sliding Window.explanation: Since k = 1, we consider each element of the array separately and compare it to lower and upper. 1 < 3 (-1 point), 2 < 3 (-1 point), 3 == 3 (0 points), 4 > 3 (+1 point), 5 > 3 (+1 point). Total points = 0.

Stack.title: Valid Parentheses
Stack.problem_statement: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isValid(string s) { stack<char> st; for(char c:s) { if(c=='(') st.push(')'); else if(c=='{') st.push('}'); else if(c=='[') st.push(']'); else if(st.empty() || st.top()!=c) return false; else st.pop(); } return st.empty(); } };
code.java: class Solution { public boolean isValid(String s) { Stack<Character> st = new Stack<>(); for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(c=='(') st.push(')'); else if(c=='{') st.push('}'); else if(c=='[') st.push(']'); else if(st.isEmpty() || st.pop()!=c) return false; } return st.isEmpty(); } }

Stack.title: Min Stack
Stack.problem_statement: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
Stack.explanation: MinStack retrieves the minimum value properly.

code.cpp: class MinStack { stack<int> s, m; public: void push(int val) { s.push(val); if(m.empty() || val<=m.top()) m.push(val); } void pop() { if(s.top()==m.top()) m.pop(); s.pop(); } int top() { return s.top(); } int getMin() { return m.top(); } };
code.java: class MinStack { Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(), m = new Stack<>(); public void push(int val) { s.push(val); if(m.isEmpty() || val<=m.peek()) m.push(val); } public void pop() { if(s.pop().equals(m.peek())) m.pop(); } public int top() { return s.peek(); } public int getMin() { return m.peek(); } }

Stack.title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
Stack.problem_statement: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation. Valid operators are +, -, *, and /.
constraints: tokens[i] is either an operator or an integer

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int evalRPN(vector<string>& t) { stack<long> s; for(auto& x:t) { if(x=="+"||x=="-"||x=="*"||x=="/") { long b=s.top(); s.pop(); long a=s.top(); s.pop(); if(x=="+") s.push(a+b); else if(x=="-") s.push(a-b); else if(x=="*") s.push(a*b); else s.push(a/b); } else s.push(stol(x)); } return s.top(); } };
code.java: class Solution { public int evalRPN(String[] t) { Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(String x:t) { if(x.equals("+")||x.equals("-")||x.equals("*")||x.equals("/")) { int b=s.pop(), a=s.pop(); if(x.equals("+")) s.push(a+b); else if(x.equals("-")) s.push(a-b); else if(x.equals("*")) s.push(a*b); else s.push(a/b); } else s.push(Integer.parseInt(x)); } return s.pop(); } }

Stack.title: Daily Temperatures
Stack.problem_statement: Given an array of integers temperatures, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after the ith day to get a warmer temperature.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> dailyTemperatures(vector<int>& T) { vector<int> res(T.size()); stack<int> s; for(int i=0; i<T.size(); ++i) { while(!s.empty() && T[s.top()] < T[i]) { res[s.top()] = i - s.top(); s.pop(); } s.push(i); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] dailyTemperatures(int[] T) { int[] res = new int[T.length]; Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<T.length; i++) { while(!s.isEmpty() && T[s.peek()] < T[i]) { int idx = s.pop(); res[idx] = i - idx; } s.push(i); } return res; } }

Stack.title: Next Greater Element I
Stack.problem_statement: The next greater element of some element x in an array is the first greater element that is to the right of x in the same array.
Stack.explanation: Next greater elements for nums1 elements in nums2.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> nextGreaterElement(vector<int>& n1, vector<int>& n2) { unordered_map<int,int> m; stack<int> s; for(int n : n2) { while(!s.empty() && s.top() < n) { m[s.top()] = n; s.pop(); } s.push(n); } vector<int> res; for(int n : n1) res.push_back(m.count(n) ? m[n] : -1); return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] nextGreaterElement(int[] n1, int[] n2) { Map<Integer, Integer> m = new HashMap<>(); Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int n : n2) { while(!s.isEmpty() && s.peek() < n) m.put(s.pop(), n); s.push(n); } int[] res = new int[n1.length]; for(int i=0; i<n1.length; i++) res[i] = m.getOrDefault(n1[i], -1); return res; } }

Stack.title: Next Greater Element II
Stack.problem_statement: Given a circular integer array nums, return the next greater element for every element in nums.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> nextGreaterElements(vector<int>& nums) { int n = nums.size(); vector<int> res(n, -1); stack<int> s; for(int i=0; i<2*n; i++) { while(!s.empty() && nums[s.top()] < nums[i%n]) { res[s.top()] = nums[i%n]; s.pop(); } s.push(i%n); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] nextGreaterElements(int[] nums) { int n = nums.length; int[] res = new int[n]; Arrays.fill(res, -1); Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<2*n; i++) { while(!s.isEmpty() && nums[s.peek()] < nums[i%n]) res[s.pop()] = nums[i%n]; s.push(i%n); } return res; } }

Stack.title: Backspace String Compare
Stack.problem_statement: Given two strings s and t, return true if they are equal when both are typed into empty text editors. '#' means a backspace character.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: bool backspaceCompare(string s, string t) { return build(s) == build(t); } string build(string s) { string res; for(char c:s) { if(c!='#') res+=c; else if(!res.empty()) res.pop_back(); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public boolean backspaceCompare(String s, String t) { return build(s).equals(build(t)); } private String build(String s) { Stack<Character> st = new Stack<>(); for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(c!='#') st.push(c); else if(!st.isEmpty()) st.pop(); } return String.valueOf(st); } }

Stack.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
Stack.problem_statement: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar height where the width of each bar is 1, return the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.
Stack.explanation: Largest rectangle is shown with area = 10.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int largestRectangleArea(vector<int>& H) { int res=0; H.push_back(0); stack<int> s; for(int i=0;i<H.size();i++) { while(!s.empty() && H[s.top()]>H[i]) { int h = H[s.top()]; s.pop(); int w = s.empty() ? i : i-s.top()-1; res = max(res, h*w); } s.push(i); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int largestRectangleArea(int[] heights) { int res = 0, n = heights.length; Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<=n; i++) { int h = (i==n) ? 0 : heights[i]; while(!s.isEmpty() && heights[s.peek()] > h) { int cur = heights[s.pop()]; int w = s.isEmpty() ? i : i-s.peek()-1; res = Math.max(res, cur*w); } s.push(i); } return res; } }

Stack.title: Car Fleet
Stack.problem_statement: There are n cars going to the same destination along a one-lane road. The destination is target. Return the number of car fleets that will arrive at the destination.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int carFleet(int target, vector<int>& p, vector<int>& s) { map<int, double> m; for(int i=0; i<p.size(); i++) m[-p[i]] = (double)(target-p[i])/s[i]; int res=0; double cur=0; for(auto it:m) { if(it.second > cur) { cur = it.second; res++; } } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int carFleet(int target, int[] p, int[] s) { TreeMap<Integer, Double> m = new TreeMap<>(Collections.reverseOrder()); for(int i=0; i<p.length; i++) m.put(p[i], (double)(target-p[i])/s[i]); int res=0; double cur=0; for(double t:m.values()) { if(t > cur) { cur = t; res++; } } return res; } }

Stack.title: Online Stock Span
Stack.problem_statement: Design a class that collects daily price quotes for some stock and returns the span of that stock's price for the current day.

code.cpp: class StockSpanner { stack<pair<int,int>> s; public: int next(int price) { int res=1; while(!s.empty() && s.top().first<=price) { res+=s.top().second; s.pop(); } s.push({price,res}); return res; } };
code.java: class StockSpanner { Stack<int[]> s = new Stack<>(); public int next(int price) { int res=1; while(!s.isEmpty() && s.peek()[0]<=price) res+=s.pop()[1]; s.push(new int[]{price, res}); return res; } }

Stack.title: Asteroid Collision
Stack.problem_statement: We are given an array asteroids of integers representing asteroids in a row. Find out the state of the asteroids after all collisions.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> asteroidCollision(vector<int>& a) { vector<int> s; for(int x:a) { bool add = true; while(!s.empty() && s.back()>0 && x<0) { if(s.back() < -x) { s.pop_back(); continue; } else if(s.back() == -x) s.pop_back(); add = false; break; } if(add) s.push_back(x); } return s; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] asteroidCollision(int[] a) { Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int x:a) { boolean add = true; while(!s.isEmpty() && s.peek()>0 && x<0) { if(s.peek() < -x) { s.pop(); continue; } else if(s.peek() == -x) s.pop(); add = false; break; } if(add) s.push(x); } return s.stream().mapToInt(i->i).toArray(); } }

Stack.title: Basic Calculator
Stack.problem_statement: Given a string s representing a valid expression, implement a basic calculator to evaluate it, and return the result of the evaluation.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int calculate(string s) { int sum=0, sign=1; stack<int> st; for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) { if(isdigit(s[i])) { int num=0; while(i<s.length() && isdigit(s[i])) num=num*10+(s[i++]-'0'); sum+=num*sign; i--; } else if(s[i]=='+') sign=1; else if(s[i]=='-') sign=-1; else if(s[i]=='(') { st.push(sum); st.push(sign); sum=0; sign=1; } else if(s[i]==')') { sum*=st.top(); st.pop(); sum+=st.top(); st.pop(); } } return sum; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int calculate(String s) { int sum=0, sign=1; Stack<Integer> st = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) { char c=s.charAt(i); if(Character.isDigit(c)) { int num=0; while(i<s.length() && Character.isDigit(s.charAt(i))) num=num*10+(s.charAt(i++)-'0'); sum+=num*sign; i--; } else if(c=='+') sign=1; else if(c=='-') sign=-1; else if(c=='(') { st.push(sum); st.push(sign); sum=0; sign=1; } else if(c==')') { sum*=st.pop(); sum+=st.pop(); } } return sum; } }

Stack.title: Basic Calculator II
Stack.problem_statement: Given a string s which represents an expression, evaluate this expression and return its value. It contains +,-,*,/.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int calculate(string s) { stack<int> st; int num=0; char sign='+'; for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) { if(isdigit(s[i])) num=num*10+(s[i]-'0'); if((!isdigit(s[i]) && s[i]!=' ') || i==s.length()-1) { if(sign=='+') st.push(num); else if(sign=='-') st.push(-num); else if(sign=='*') { int t=st.top()*num; st.pop(); st.push(t); } else { int t=st.top()/num; st.pop(); st.push(t); } sign=s[i]; num=0; } } int res=0; while(!st.empty()){res+=st.top(); st.pop();} return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int calculate(String s) { Stack<Integer> st = new Stack<>(); int num=0; char sign='+'; for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) { if(Character.isDigit(s.charAt(i))) num=num*10+s.charAt(i)-'0'; if((!Character.isDigit(s.charAt(i)) && s.charAt(i)!=' ') || i==s.length()-1) { if(sign=='+') st.push(num); else if(sign=='-') st.push(-num); else if(sign=='*') st.push(st.pop()*num); else if(sign=='/') st.push(st.pop()/num); sign=s.charAt(i); num=0; } } int res=0; for(int i:st) res+=i; return res; } }

Stack.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
Stack.problem_statement: Implement a first in first out (FIFO) queue using only two stacks.

code.cpp: class MyQueue { stack<int> in, out; public: void push(int x) { in.push(x); } int pop() { peek(); int val=out.top(); out.pop(); return val; } int peek() { if(out.empty()) while(!in.empty()){ out.push(in.top()); in.pop(); } return out.top(); } bool empty() { return in.empty() && out.empty(); } };
code.java: class MyQueue { Stack<Integer> in = new Stack<>(), out = new Stack<>(); public void push(int x) { in.push(x); } public int pop() { peek(); return out.pop(); } public int peek() { if(out.isEmpty()) while(!in.isEmpty()) out.push(in.pop()); return out.peek(); } public boolean empty() { return in.isEmpty() && out.isEmpty(); } }

Stack.title: Implement Stack using Queues
Stack.problem_statement: Implement a last-in-first-out (LIFO) stack using only two queues.

code.cpp: class MyStack { queue<int> q; public: void push(int x) { q.push(x); for(int i=0; i<q.size()-1; i++) { q.push(q.front()); q.pop(); } } int pop() { int v=q.front(); q.pop(); return v; } int top() { return q.front(); } bool empty() { return q.empty(); } };
code.java: class MyStack { Queue<Integer> q = new LinkedList<>(); public void push(int x) { q.add(x); for(int i=0; i<q.size()-1; i++) q.add(q.poll()); } public int pop() { return q.poll(); } public int top() { return q.peek(); } public boolean empty() { return q.isEmpty(); } }

Stack.title: Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String
Stack.problem_statement: You are given a string s consisting of lowercase English letters. A duplicate removal consists of choosing two adjacent and equal letters and removing them.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: string removeDuplicates(string s) { string res; for(char c:s) { if(!res.empty() && res.back()==c) res.pop_back(); else res.push_back(c); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public String removeDuplicates(String s) { StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(sb.length()>0 && sb.charAt(sb.length()-1)==c) sb.deleteCharAt(sb.length()-1); else sb.append(c); } return sb.toString(); } }

Stack.title: Remove All Adjacent Duplicates in String II
Stack.problem_statement: You are given a string s and an integer k, a k duplicate removal consists of choosing k adjacent and equal letters from s and removing them.
Stack.explanation: Remove duplicates by groups of k.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: string removeDuplicates(string s, int k) { vector<pair<char,int>> st; for(char c:s) { if(!st.empty() && st.back().first==c) st.back().second++; else st.push_back({c,1}); if(st.back().second==k) st.pop_back(); } string res; for(auto p:st) res+=string(p.second, p.first); return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public String removeDuplicates(String s, int k) { Stack<int[]> st = new Stack<>(); for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(!st.isEmpty() && st.peek()[0]==c) st.peek()[1]++; else st.push(new int[]{c, 1}); if(st.peek()[1]==k) st.pop(); } StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); for(int[] a:st) for(int i=0; i<a[1]; i++) sb.append((char)a[0]); return sb.toString(); } }

Stack.title: Decode String
Stack.problem_statement: Given an encoded string, return its decoded string. The encoding rule is: k[encoded_string].
Stack.explanation: a is repeated 3 times, bc is repeated 2 times.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: string decodeString(string s) { stack<int> ns; stack<string> ss; string res; int n=0; for(char c:s) { if(isdigit(c)) n=n*10+(c-'0'); else if(isalpha(c)) res+=c; else if(c=='[') { ns.push(n); ss.push(res); n=0; res=""; } else { string t=ss.top(); ss.pop(); for(int i=0; i<ns.top(); i++) t+=res; ns.pop(); res=t; } } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public String decodeString(String s) { Stack<Integer> ns = new Stack<>(); Stack<StringBuilder> ss = new Stack<>(); StringBuilder res = new StringBuilder(); int n=0; for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(Character.isDigit(c)) n=n*10+(c-'0'); else if(c=='[') { ns.push(n); ss.push(res); n=0; res = new StringBuilder(); } else if(c==']') { StringBuilder t = ss.pop(); int k = ns.pop(); for(int i=0; i<k; i++) t.append(res); res = t; } else res.append(c); } return res.toString(); } }

Stack.title: Score of Parentheses
Stack.problem_statement: Given a balanced parentheses string s, return the score of the string. () has score 1. AB has score A + B. (A) has score 2 * A.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int scoreOfParentheses(string s) { stack<int> st; st.push(0); for(char c:s) { if(c=='(') st.push(0); else { int v = st.top(); st.pop(); st.top() += max(2*v, 1); } } return st.top(); } };
code.java: class Solution { public int scoreOfParentheses(String s) { Stack<Integer> st = new Stack<>(); st.push(0); for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(c=='(') st.push(0); else { int v = st.pop(); int w = st.pop(); st.push(w + Math.max(2*v, 1)); } } return st.pop(); } }

Stack.title: Minimum Add to Make Parentheses Valid
Stack.problem_statement: You are given a parentheses string s. In one move, you can insert a parenthesis at any position. Return the minimum number of moves required to make s valid.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int minAddToMakeValid(string s) { int l=0, r=0; for(char c:s) { if(c=='(') l++; else if(l>0) l--; else r++; } return l+r; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int minAddToMakeValid(String s) { int l=0, r=0; for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { if(c=='(') l++; else if(l>0) l--; else r++; } return l+r; } }

Stack.title: Minimum Remove to Make Valid Parentheses
Stack.problem_statement: Given a string s of '(' , ')' and lowercase English characters. Your task is to remove the minimum number of parentheses to make the string valid.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: string minRemoveToMakeValid(string s) { stack<int> st; for(int i=0; i<s.length(); i++) { if(s[i]=='(') st.push(i); else if(s[i]==')') { if(!st.empty()) st.pop(); else s[i]='*'; } } while(!st.empty()){s[st.top()]='*'; st.pop();} string res; for(char c:s) if(c!='*') res+=c; return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public String minRemoveToMakeValid(String s) { char[] arr = s.toCharArray(); Stack<Integer> st = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<arr.length; i++) { if(arr[i]=='(') st.push(i); else if(arr[i]==')') { if(!st.isEmpty()) st.pop(); else arr[i]='*'; } } while(!st.isEmpty()) arr[st.pop()]='*'; StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); for(char c:arr) if(c!='*') sb.append(c); return sb.toString(); } }

Stack.title: Valid Parenthesis String
Stack.problem_statement: Given a string s containing only three types of characters: '(', ')' and '*', return true if s is valid. '*' can represent '(' or ')' or empty.
Stack.explanation: Valid using * as empty or parentheses.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: bool checkValidString(string s) { int low=0, high=0; for(char c:s) { low += (c=='(')?1:-1; high += (c!=')')?1:-1; if(high<0) return false; low = max(low, 0); } return low==0; } };
code.java: class Solution { public boolean checkValidString(String s) { int low=0, high=0; for(char c:s.toCharArray()) { low += (c=='(')?1:-1; high += (c!=')')?1:-1; if(high<0) return false; low = Math.max(low, 0); } return low==0; } }

Stack.title: Remove K Digits
Stack.problem_statement: Given string num representing a non-negative integer num, and an integer k, return the smallest possible integer after removing k digits from num.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: string removeKdigits(string num, int k) { string res=""; for(char c:num) { while(k>0 && res.length() && res.back()>c) { res.pop_back(); k--; } res.push_back(c); } while(k>0 && res.length()) { res.pop_back(); k--; } int i=0; while(i<res.length() && res[i]=='0') i++; res=res.substr(i); return res.length() ? res : "0"; } };
code.java: class Solution { public String removeKdigits(String num, int k) { Stack<Character> st = new Stack<>(); for(char c:num.toCharArray()) { while(k>0 && !st.isEmpty() && st.peek()>c) { st.pop(); k--; } st.push(c); } while(k>0) { st.pop(); k--; } StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); for(char c:st) sb.append(c); while(sb.length()>0 && sb.charAt(0)=='0') sb.deleteCharAt(0); return sb.length()==0 ? "0" : sb.toString(); } }

Stack.title: 132 Pattern
Stack.problem_statement: Given an array of n integers nums, a 132 pattern is a subsequence of three integers nums[i], nums[j] and nums[k] such that i < j < k and nums[i] < nums[k] < nums[j].

code.cpp: class Solution { public: bool find132pattern(vector<int>& nums) { int s3=INT_MIN; stack<int> st; for(int i=nums.size()-1; i>=0; i--) { if(nums[i] < s3) return true; while(!st.empty() && nums[i]>st.top()) { s3=st.top(); st.pop(); } st.push(nums[i]); } return false; } };
code.java: class Solution { public boolean find132pattern(int[] nums) { int s3 = Integer.MIN_VALUE; Stack<Integer> st = new Stack<>(); for(int i=nums.length-1; i>=0; i--) { if(nums[i] < s3) return true; while(!st.isEmpty() && nums[i]>st.peek()) s3 = st.pop(); st.push(nums[i]); } return false; } }

Stack.title: Simplify Path
Stack.problem_statement: Given a string path, which is an absolute path to a file or directory in a Unix-style file system, convert it to the simplified canonical path.
Stack.explanation: Simplify removes trailing slashes and redundant slashes.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: string simplifyPath(string path) { stack<string> st; string dir; stringstream ss(path); while(getline(ss, dir, '/')) { if(dir=="" || dir==".") continue; if(dir=="..") { if(!st.empty()) st.pop(); } else st.push(dir); } string res=""; while(!st.empty()){res="/"+st.top()+res; st.pop();} return res.empty() ? "/" : res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public String simplifyPath(String path) { Stack<String> st = new Stack<>(); for(String dir:path.split("/")) { if(dir.equals("..") && !st.isEmpty()) st.pop(); else if(!Arrays.asList("", ".", "..").contains(dir)) st.push(dir); } return "/" + String.join("/", st); } }

Stack.title: Flatten Nested List Iterator
Stack.problem_statement: You are given a nested list of integers nestedList. Each element is either an integer or a list. Implement an iterator to flatten it.

code.cpp: class NestedIterator { stack<NestedInteger*> st; public: NestedIterator(vector<NestedInteger> &L) { for(int i=L.size()-1; i>=0; i--) st.push(&L[i]); } int next() { int v = st.top()->getInteger(); st.pop(); return v; } bool hasNext() { while(!st.empty()) { auto x=st.top(); if(x->isInteger()) return true; st.pop(); auto &L = x->getList(); for(int i=L.size()-1; i>=0; i--) st.push(&L[i]); } return false; } };
code.java: public class NestedIterator implements Iterator<Integer> { Stack<NestedInteger> st = new Stack<>(); public NestedIterator(List<NestedInteger> L) { for(int i=L.size()-1; i>=0; i--) st.push(L.get(i)); } @Override public Integer next() { return st.pop().getInteger(); } @Override public boolean hasNext() { while(!st.isEmpty()) { if(st.peek().isInteger()) return true; List<NestedInteger> L = st.pop().getList(); for(int i=L.size()-1; i>=0; i--) st.push(L.get(i)); } return false; } }

Stack.title: Exclusive Time of Functions
Stack.problem_statement: Given an integer n denoting the number of functions and an array logs, return the exclusive time of each function in an array.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> exclusiveTime(int n, vector<string>& logs) { vector<int> res(n, 0); stack<pair<int,int>> st; for(auto log:logs) { int c1=log.find(':'), c2=log.rfind(':'); int id=stoi(log.substr(0,c1)), t=stoi(log.substr(c2+1)); string type=log.substr(c1+1, c2-c1-1); if(type=="start") st.push({id,t}); else { int len = t-st.top().second+1; res[st.top().first]+=len; st.pop(); if(!st.empty()) res[st.top().first]-=len; } } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int[] exclusiveTime(int n, List<String> logs) { int[] res = new int[n]; Stack<int[]> st = new Stack<>(); for(String log:logs) { String[] p = log.split(":"); int id = Integer.parseInt(p[0]), t = Integer.parseInt(p[2]); if(p[1].equals("start")) st.push(new int[]{id,t}); else { int len = t-st.pop()[1]+1; res[id]+=len; if(!st.isEmpty()) res[st.peek()[0]]-=len; } } return res; } }

Stack.title: Design a Stack With Increment Operation
Stack.problem_statement: Design a stack which supports push, pop, and an operation to increment the bottom k elements of the stack.

code.cpp: class CustomStack { vector<int> st, inc; int max_sz; public: CustomStack(int sz) { max_sz=sz; } void push(int x) { if(st.size()<max_sz) { st.push_back(x); inc.push_back(0); } } int pop() { int i=st.size()-1; if(i<0) return -1; if(i>0) inc[i-1]+=inc[i]; int res = st[i]+inc[i]; st.pop_back(); inc.pop_back(); return res; } void increment(int k, int v) { int i = min(k, (int)st.size()) - 1; if(i>=0) inc[i]+=v; } };
code.java: class CustomStack { int[] st, inc; int top = -1; public CustomStack(int sz) { st = new int[sz]; inc = new int[sz]; } public void push(int x) { if(top+1 < st.length) st[++top] = x; } public int pop() { if(top<0) return -1; int res = st[top] + inc[top]; if(top>0) inc[top-1] += inc[top]; inc[top] = 0; return res; } public void increment(int k, int v) { int i = Math.min(k-1, top); if(i>=0) inc[i] += v; } }

Stack.title: Maximal Rectangle
Stack.problem_statement: Given a rows x cols binary matrix filled with 0's and 1's, find the largest rectangle containing only 1's and return its area.
Stack.explanation: Uses largest rectangle in histogram method row by row.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int maximalRectangle(vector<vector<char>>& m) { if(m.empty()) return 0; int res=0; vector<int> h(m[0].size()+1, 0); for(auto& r:m) { for(int i=0; i<m[0].size(); i++) h[i]=r[i]=='1'?h[i]+1:0; stack<int> s; for(int i=0; i<h.size(); i++) { while(!s.empty() && h[s.top()]>h[i]) { int ht=h[s.top()]; s.pop(); res=max(res, ht*(s.empty()?i:i-s.top()-1)); } s.push(i); } } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int maximalRectangle(char[][] m) { if(m.length==0) return 0; int res=0; int[] h = new int[m[0].length+1]; for(char[] r:m) { for(int i=0; i<m[0].length; i++) h[i]=r[i]=='1'?h[i]+1:0; Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<h.length; i++) { while(!s.isEmpty() && h[s.peek()]>h[i]) { int ht=h[s.pop()]; res=Math.max(res, ht*(s.isEmpty()?i:i-s.peek()-1)); } s.push(i); } } return res; } }

Stack.title: Trapping Rain Water
Stack.problem_statement: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

code.cpp: class Solution { public: int trap(vector<int>& H) { int res=0; stack<int> s; for(int i=0; i<H.size(); i++) { while(!s.empty() && H[i]>H[s.top()]) { int t=s.top(); s.pop(); if(s.empty()) break; res += (min(H[i], H[s.top()])-H[t])*(i-s.top()-1); } s.push(i); } return res; } };
code.java: class Solution { public int trap(int[] H) { int res=0; Stack<Integer> s = new Stack<>(); for(int i=0; i<H.length; i++) { while(!s.isEmpty() && H[i]>H[s.peek()]) { int t=s.pop(); if(s.isEmpty()) break; res += (Math.min(H[i], H[s.peek()])-H[t])*(i-s.peek()-1); } s.push(i); } return res; } }

Strings.title: String to Integer (atoi)
Strings.problem_statement: Implement the myAtoi(string s) function, which converts a string to a 32-bit signed integer.
Strings.constraints: 0 <= s.length <= 200, s consists of English letters (lower-case and upper-case), digits (0-9), ' ', '+', '-', and '.'.
Strings.explanation: The first non-whitespace character is '-', which is the minus sign. Then take as many numerical digits as possible, which gets 42.

code.python:
class Solution:
    def myAtoi(self, s: str) -> int:
        s = s.lstrip()
        if not s: return 0
        sign = -1 if s[0] == '-' else 1
        if s[0] in ['-', '+']: s = s[1:]
        res = 0
        for c in s:
            if not c.isdigit(): break
            res = res * 10 + int(c)
        res *= sign
        if res > 2**31 - 1: return 2**31 - 1
        if res < -2**31: return -2**31
        return res

Strings.title: Add Strings
Strings.problem_statement: Given two non-negative integers, num1 and num2 represented as string, return the sum of num1 and num2 as a string.
Strings.constraints: 1 <= num1.length, num2.length <= 10^4. They consist of only digits and don't have leading zeros.

code.python:
class Solution:
    def addStrings(self, num1: str, num2: str) -> str:
        res = []
        carry = 0
        p1 = len(num1) - 1
        p2 = len(num2) - 1
        while p1 >= 0 or p2 >= 0 or carry:
            x1 = int(num1[p1]) if p1 >= 0 else 0
            x2 = int(num2[p2]) if p2 >= 0 else 0
            value = (x1 + x2 + carry) % 10
            carry = (x1 + x2 + carry) // 10
            res.append(value)
            p1 -= 1
            p2 -= 1
        return ''.join(str(x) for x in res)[::-1]

Strings.title: Permutation in String
Strings.problem_statement: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise.
Strings.constraints: 1 <= s1.length, s2.length <= 10^4, s1 and s2 consist of lowercase English letters.
Strings.explanation: s2 contains one permutation of s1 ("ba").

code.python:
class Solution:
    def checkInclusion(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> bool:
        if len(s1) > len(s2): return False
        s1Count, s2Count = [0] * 26, [0] * 26
        for i in range(len(s1)):
            s1Count[ord(s1[i]) - ord('a')] += 1
            s2Count[ord(s2[i]) - ord('a')] += 1
        matches = 0
        for i in range(26):
            matches += (1 if s1Count[i] == s2Count[i] else 0)
        l = 0
        for r in range(len(s1), len(s2)):
            if matches == 26: return True
            index = ord(s2[r]) - ord('a')
            s2Count[index] += 1
            if s1Count[index] == s2Count[index]: matches += 1
            elif s1Count[index] + 1 == s2Count[index]: matches -= 1
            index = ord(s2[l]) - ord('a')
            s2Count[index] -= 1
            if s1Count[index] == s2Count[index]: matches += 1
            elif s1Count[index] - 1 == s2Count[index]: matches -= 1
            l += 1
        return matches == 26

Trees.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
Trees.problem_statement: A path in a binary tree is a sequence of nodes where each pair of adjacent nodes in the sequence has an edge connecting them. Return the maximum path sum of any non-empty path.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 3 * 10^4].
Trees.explanation: The optimal path is 15 -> 20 -> 7 with a path sum of 15 + 20 + 7 = 42.

code.python:
class Solution:
    def maxPathSum(self, root: Optional[TreeNode]) -> int:
        self.max_sum = float('-inf')
        def dfs(node):
            if not node: return 0
            left = max(0, dfs(node.left))
            right = max(0, dfs(node.right))
            self.max_sum = max(self.max_sum, left + right + node.val)
            return max(left, right) + node.val
        dfs(root)
        return self.max_sum
